name: Solid
description: A declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces with fine-grained reactivity

description.value: A declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces with fine-grained reactivity

initial-release.comment: Initial development started in 2018; v1.0 released in 2021

active-maintenance.comment: Regular commits and releases; 1,859 total commits on main branch

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity with signals; compiles templates to real DOM nodes without Virtual DOM

component-model.comment: Function components that execute once to set up the view; render-once mental model

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative JSX-based rendering with fine-grained reactive updates

state-management-approach.comment: Comprehensive built-in state with signals, stores, and context API

opinionatedness.comment: Library approach similar to React; provides primitives without prescribing architecture

backward-compatibility.comment: Stable API since 1.0; focus on stability with gradual improvements

compiles-to.comment: Compiles JSX to optimized JavaScript that directly manipulates the DOM

typescript-support.comment: Written in TypeScript with full type definitions included

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; works with CSS modules, Tailwind, CSS-in-JS libraries

build-required.comment: Build step required for JSX compilation; no CDN-only option for production

reactive-primitives.comment: createSignal, createStore for reactive state primitives

computed-values.comment: createMemo for derived/computed values that auto-update

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow; uses controlled inputs with signal setters

event-handling.comment: onClick, onInput, etc. with automatic event delegation for performance

conditional-rendering.comment: Show and Switch/Match components; ternary operators in JSX

list-rendering.comment: For and Index components for efficient list rendering

component-slots.comment: props.children and named slots via props

teleport-portal.comment: Portal component for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: Suspense component for async loading boundaries

error-boundaries.comment: ErrorBoundary component for catching and handling errors

context-provide-inject.comment: createContext and useContext for dependency injection

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: ref directive for direct DOM element access

official-router.comment: Solid Router is official first-party routing solution

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Built-in lazy loading and preloading capabilities

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll position management via useLocation

built-in-global-state.comment: createStore and context API for app-wide state management

global-state-library.comment: Built-in stores sufficient for most cases; no separate official library needed

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Solid DevTools browser extension for state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via community libraries

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not natively supported; fine-grained reactivity makes traditional time-travel less applicable

official-cli.comment: SolidStart provides project scaffolding via npm create solid

browser-devtools.comment: Solid DevTools Chrome extension for debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: Solid extension for VS Code with syntax highlighting and IntelliSense

error-messages.comment: Good error messages with component context; compiler catches many issues at build time

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs at docs.solidjs.com with guides, API reference, and examples

bundle-size-core.comment: ~8.54 kB minified + gzipped for solid-js core

bundle-size-full.comment: ~15 kB minified + gzipped with solid-js/web and router; varies by usage due to tree-shaking

virtual-dom.comment: No Virtual DOM; compiles directly to real DOM operations

compile-time-optimization.comment: Compiler performs static analysis and optimizes reactive updates at build time

tree-shaking.comment: Excellent tree-shaking support; only used primitives are bundled

code-splitting.comment: Via lazy() function and dynamic imports

lazy-components.comment: lazy() for loading components on demand

ssr-capable.comment: Full SSR support via renderToString, renderToStringAsync, renderToStream

partial-hydration.comment: Progressive hydration support; SolidStart supports islands architecture

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Consistently among the fastest frameworks; geometric mean ~1.02 (lower is better, 1.0 is vanilla JS baseline)

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~1.3 million weekly downloads as of December 2025

component-libraries.comment: SUID (MUI port), Kobalte, Solid UI (shadcn port), Flowbite Solid, Ark UI, Solid Blocks, Solibee, Hope UI

meta-framework.comment: Official full-stack meta-framework with SSR, file-based routing, and API routes

major-companies-using.comment: Growing adoption; Google Chrome team sponsors development

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Ryan Carniato works at Netlify; Google Chrome Aurora fund sponsors performance work

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Growing community; approximately 1,500 questions tagged solid-js

job-market-demand.comment: Growing adoption; niche but increasing demand, especially in performance-critical applications

testing-library-name.comment: Official @solidjs/testing-library; Vitest recommended as test runner

component-testing.comment: Via @solidjs/testing-library with render and renderHook utilities

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress for end-to-end testing

mobile-framework.comment: No official native mobile framework; can use with Tauri or Capacitor for hybrid apps

pwa-support.comment: PWA support via Vite PWA plugin or Workbox integration

desktop-framework.comment: Works well with Tauri for desktop applications; no Electron-specific integration

learning-curve.comment: Gentle learning curve for React developers; JSX familiar but reactivity model requires mental shift

concepts-to-learn.comment: Signals, Effects, Memos, Stores, JSX control flow (Show, For), Context

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~3 minutes to scaffold with npm create solid and run first component

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides from React or other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be embedded in existing applications; similar to React's adoption model
